_disclaimer: APPROXIMATE DATA — Tax incentive programs change frequently. Data is from public state legislation and economic development websites. Verify with state officials before making investment decisions.
_sources: National Conference of State Legislatures (ncsl.org) - public
State economic development agency websites - public
Data Center Knowledge news analysis - public
State legislature bill tracking websites - public

tax_incentives.program_name: Virginia Data Center Investment Grant
tax_incentives.notes: Machinery/equipment sales tax exempt; grant up to $35M for qualifying projects

tax_incentives.program_name: Oregon Enterprise Zone / Data Center Tax Exemption
tax_incentives.notes: Up to 5-year property tax exemption in enterprise zones; Morrow, Umatilla, Hood River counties

tax_incentives.program_name: Washington High-Tech Sales Tax Deferral
tax_incentives.incentive_type: Sales Tax Deferral + B&O Tax Credit
tax_incentives.notes: Sales tax deferral on construction; B&O tax reduction for rural counties

tax_incentives.program_name: Iowa Data Center Sales Tax Exemption
tax_incentives.incentive_type: Sales Tax Exemption + Property Tax Abatement
tax_incentives.notes: 100% sales tax exemption on DP equipment; up to 20-year property tax abatement

tax_incentives.program_name: Arizona Data Center Tax Incentive
tax_incentives.notes: Transaction privilege tax exemption on equipment; property tax valuation reduction

tax_incentives.program_name: Texas Chapter 313 / Economic Development
tax_incentives.incentive_type: Property Tax Limitation + Sales Tax Exemption
tax_incentives.notes: Sales tax exemption on DC hardware; local school district property tax limitations

tax_incentives.program_name: Georgia Data Center Tax Exemption
tax_incentives.notes: Sales tax exemption on computer equipment and electricity for qualifying DCs

tax_incentives.program_name: Nevada Data Center Abatement Program
tax_incentives.incentive_type: Sales/Use Tax Abatement + Property Tax Abatement
tax_incentives.notes: Up to 75% sales tax abatement; 50% personal property tax abatement for 10 years

tax_incentives.program_name: Ohio Data Center Tax Exemption
tax_incentives.incentive_type: Sales Tax Exemption + Job Creation Tax Credit
tax_incentives.notes: Sales tax exemption on equipment; job creation tax credit through Ohio Development

tax_incentives.program_name: Indiana EDGE Tax Credit / IT Equipment Exemption
tax_incentives.notes: IT equipment sales tax exemption; EDGE credit for job creation

tax_incentives.program_name: North Carolina Data Center Tax Incentive
tax_incentives.notes: Sales tax refund on server equipment; local property tax incentive agreements

tax_incentives.program_name: South Carolina Data Center Incentive Program
tax_incentives.incentive_type: Sales Tax Exemption + Jobs Tax Credit
tax_incentives.notes: Full sales tax exemption on equipment; jobs tax credit $1,500-8,000 per job

tax_incentives.program_name: Nebraska ImagiNE Act Data Center Incentive
tax_incentives.notes: Sales tax exemption; refundable income tax credit based on investment/jobs

tax_incentives.program_name: Wyoming Data Center Tax Policy
tax_incentives.incentive_type: Low Property Tax + No Income Tax
tax_incentives.notes: No state income tax; low property tax rates; competitive power costs

tax_incentives.program_name: Illinois Data Center Investment Credit
tax_incentives.notes: 20% investment tax credit on qualifying spend; expanded 2021

tax_incentives.program_name: Tennessee FastTrack Infrastructure + Sales Tax Exemption
tax_incentives.notes: Industrial machinery exemption; FastTrack job training grants

tax_incentives.program_name: Kentucky Economic Development Incentives
tax_incentives.notes: Machinery and equipment sales tax exemption; KEDFA incentive program

tax_incentives.program_name: Wisconsin Economic Development Tax Credit
tax_incentives.notes: Enterprise zone tax credits; sales tax exemptions on qualifying equipment

tax_incentives.program_name: Colorado OEDIT Advanced Industry Grant
tax_incentives.notes: Advanced industry grant program; enterprise zone investment tax credits

tax_incentives.program_name: Missouri Works + Data Center Incentives
tax_incentives.notes: Missouri Works job creation incentive; computer equipment sales tax exemption

tax_incentives.program_name: Data Center Equipment and Electricity Sales Tax Exemption (HB 7063)
tax_incentives.exemption_scope: Sales and use tax on computer equipment, cooling systems, and electricity used by qualifying data centers.
tax_incentives.notes: HB 7063 signed into law July 2023. 20-year exemption for facilities meeting $150M capital investment threshold.
tax_incentives.source_label: Florida Department of Revenue — Data Center Exemptions

tax_incentives.program_name: Oklahoma Data Center Sales and Use Tax Exemption (68 O.S. §1359.2)
tax_incentives.exemption_scope: Full sales and use tax exemption on data center equipment and electricity for qualifying facilities.
tax_incentives.notes: Oklahoma's data center exemption has driven significant Microsoft and other hyperscale investment in OKC metro.
tax_incentives.source_label: Oklahoma Tax Commission — Data Center Exemption

tax_incentives.program_name: Kentucky Data Center Sales and Use Tax Exemption (KRS 139.517)
tax_incentives.exemption_scope: Sales and use tax exemption on data center servers, cooling equipment, and electricity for qualifying facilities.
tax_incentives.notes: Kentucky expanded the program in 2020 to include all electrical costs, not just equipment.

tax_incentives.program_name: New Mexico Data Center Gross Receipts Tax Deduction (NMSA §7-9-57)
tax_incentives.exemption_scope: Gross receipts tax deduction for receipts from selling data center equipment and services to qualifying data centers.
tax_incentives.notes: New Mexico's GRT deduction applies to equipment, electricity, and cooling systems for qualifying data centers.
tax_incentives.source_label: NM Taxation and Revenue — Data Center GRT Deduction

tax_incentives.program_name: Idaho Data Center Equipment and Power Sales Tax Exemption (IC §63-3622O)
tax_incentives.exemption_scope: Permanent sales tax exemption on computers, servers, cooling equipment, and electricity for qualifying data centers.
tax_incentives.notes: Idaho exemption has no sunset clause; Meta Eagle data center campus qualifies.
tax_incentives.source_label: Idaho State Tax Commission — Data Center Exemption

tax_incentives.program_name: Michigan Data Center Sales and Use Tax Exemption (PA 328)
tax_incentives.exemption_scope: Sales and use tax exemption on servers, networking equipment, cooling, and backup power for qualifying data centers.
tax_incentives.notes: Michigan's exemption has one of the lowest investment thresholds nationally at $25M.
tax_incentives.source_label: Michigan Economic Development Corporation — Data Center Incentives

tax_incentives.program_name: Nebraska Data Center Personal Property Tax Exemption (LB 1031)
tax_incentives.exemption_scope: Personal property tax exemption for computer systems, servers, and data center equipment.
tax_incentives.notes: Nebraska LB 1031 provides blanket personal property tax exemption for data center equipment.
tax_incentives.source_label: Nebraska Department of Revenue — Data Center Tax Exemptions

tax_incentives.program_name: South Carolina Data Center Income Tax Credit (§12-6-3375)
tax_incentives.exemption_scope: Income tax credits and special source revenue bond financing for qualifying data centers.
tax_incentives.notes: SC also offers property tax abatement through Special Source Revenue Credits for qualifying facilities.
tax_incentives.source_label: SC Department of Commerce — Data Center Incentives

tax_incentives.program_name: Alabama Jobs Act Data Center Investment Incentive
tax_incentives.authority: Alabama Department of Commerce / Alabama Jobs Act (Ala. Code §40-18-376)
tax_incentives.description: The Alabama Jobs Act provides income tax credits and sales tax abatements for qualifying capital investments in Alabama, including large data centers. Projects investing $2M+ in a 12-month period in qualifying property and creating qualifying jobs may receive up to a 3% income tax credit on qualifying investment and an 80% abatement of non-educational sales tax on qualifying equipment for up to 20 years.

sources.label: Alabama Jobs Act — Code of Alabama §40-18-376

tax_incentives.program_name: Maryland Data Center Business Property Tax Credit
tax_incentives.authority: Maryland Department of Assessments and Taxation (Md. Code Ann., Tax-Prop. §9-103)
tax_incentives.description: Maryland provides a state data center equipment property tax exemption and a local personal property tax credit for qualifying data center businesses. Qualifying facilities (50,000+ sq ft, $2M+ investment) may receive exemptions on data center equipment under Md. Code Ann., Tax-Prop. §9-103 and under county-level economic development agreements.

sources.label: Maryland Data Center Business Property Tax Exemption

tax_incentives.program_name: North Dakota Data Center Property Tax Exemption
tax_incentives.description: North Dakota exempts qualifying data center equipment from state and local property taxes for up to five years. Qualifying facilities must be 25,000 sq ft or larger and represent a capital investment of at least $10 million. The exemption applies to servers, networking equipment, cooling infrastructure, and other data center equipment. Renewable energy use may extend benefits.

sources.label: ND Century Code §57-02-08.31 — Data Center Exemption

tax_incentives.program_name: Pennsylvania Data Center Equipment Sales Tax Exemption
tax_incentives.authority: Pennsylvania Revenue Code, Act 84 of 2016 (72 P.S. §7204(79))
tax_incentives.description: Pennsylvania's Act 84 of 2016 created a sales and use tax exemption for qualifying data center equipment, including servers, networking equipment, and related infrastructure purchased for use in a qualified data center. Qualifying facilities must have at least 100,000 sq ft of computer floor space and represent at least $25 million in capital investment. The exemption is capped at $5 million per year per facility.

sources.label: Pennsylvania Act 84 of 2016 — Data Center Exemption

tax_incentives.program_name: New Jersey Data Center Sales Tax Exemption (UEZ Program)
tax_incentives.authority: New Jersey Urban Enterprise Zone Act / N.J.S.A. 52:27H-60 et seq.
tax_incentives.description: New Jersey's Urban Enterprise Zone program provides a 50% reduction in sales tax on qualifying purchases by businesses in designated UEZ areas, including major data center corridors in Essex, Hudson, and Middlesex counties. Additionally, New Jersey provides corporation business tax credits for capital investment in qualified technology businesses under the Technology Business Tax Certificate Transfer Program.

tax_incentives.program_name: Minnesota Data Center Sales Tax Exemption
tax_incentives.authority: Minnesota Department of Revenue (Minn. Stat. §297A.68, Subd. 42)
tax_incentives.description: Minnesota exempts qualifying computer equipment and peripherals purchased for use in an enterprise data center from the state sales tax. Qualifying facilities must have at least 25,000 sq ft of raised floor space and represent a capital investment of at least $30 million. The exemption covers servers, networking equipment, cooling infrastructure, and power equipment. The program was enacted to attract hyperscale data center investment to the state.

sources.label: Minnesota Statutes §297A.68, Subd. 42 — Data Center Exemption

tax_incentives.program_name: Utah Data Center Equipment Sales Tax Exemption
tax_incentives.authority: Utah State Tax Commission (Utah Code Ann. §59-12-104(77))
tax_incentives.description: Utah exempts qualifying data center equipment and software from state sales and use tax under Utah Code Ann. §59-12-104(77). Qualifying facilities must meet minimum capital investment thresholds and are required to pay prevailing wages. The exemption applies to servers, networking equipment, power and cooling infrastructure. Utah also offers the EDTIF (Economic Development Tax Increment Financing) program for large capital projects.

sources.label: Utah Code Ann. §59-12-104(77) — Data Center Tax Exemption

tax_incentives.program_name: New York Data Center Tax Credit (Empire State Digital Program)
tax_incentives.authority: New York State Department of Taxation and Finance (N.Y. Tax Law §28)
tax_incentives.description: New York's Empire State Digital Program provides a refundable tax credit of up to 5% on capital investments in qualified data centers. Qualifying facilities must invest at least $100 million in capital expenditures and create or retain qualifying jobs. The credit applies to qualifying computer hardware, software, and related infrastructure. Projects must be approved by Empire State Development.

sources.label: New York Tax Law §28 — Empire State Digital Credit

tax_incentives.program_name: Arkansas Data Center Sales and Use Tax Exemption
tax_incentives.authority: Arkansas Department of Finance and Administration (Ark. Code Ann. §26-52-465)
tax_incentives.description: Arkansas provides a sales and use tax exemption for qualifying data center equipment under Ark. Code Ann. §26-52-465. Qualifying facilities must represent a capital investment of at least $25 million in a 48-month period. The exemption covers servers, storage equipment, networking infrastructure, and power and cooling equipment. Arkansas also provides income tax incentives through the ADVANTAGE Arkansas program.

sources.label: Arkansas DFA — Data Center Tax Exemption §26-52-465

tax_incentives.program_name: Connecticut Data Center Equipment Sales Tax Exemption
tax_incentives.notes: Conn. Gen. Stat. §12-412(112): Exempts servers, storage, and networking equipment purchased for use in an eligible data center from Connecticut sales and use tax. Qualifying facility must maintain a minimum of 10,000 sq ft of data center floor space. Effective since 2013 with subsequent amendments broadening eligible equipment.

tax_incentives.program_name: Florida Data Center Equipment Sales Tax Exemption
tax_incentives.notes: §212.08(7)(fff) F.S. (enacted 2021): Provides a full sales and use tax exemption for data center equipment including servers, storage, UPS systems, and cooling equipment for qualifying colocation and enterprise data centers meeting a $150M investment threshold and minimum 25 direct jobs. Applies statewide; Broward, Miami-Dade, and Orange counties are primary beneficiaries.

tax_incentives.program_name: Louisiana Quality Jobs Program — Technology Sector
tax_incentives.incentive_type: Payroll Tax Rebate + Sales Tax Rebate
tax_incentives.notes: R.S. 51:2453 (Quality Jobs Program): Provides a 6% annual payroll tax rebate for up to 10 years for qualifying technology companies creating net new jobs paying at or above the state average wage. Data center operators qualifying as technology infrastructure companies may also receive a rebate on sales taxes paid on construction materials. Requires a minimum $25M capital investment and 50 new direct jobs. LED Fast Start workforce training also available.

tax_incentives.program_name: New Hampshire Tax-Free Business Environment for Data Centers
tax_incentives.incentive_type: No Sales Tax + No Income Tax
tax_incentives.notes: New Hampshire has no general sales and use tax (RSA ch. 78-A does not apply to equipment sales) and no personal or business income tax, making all data center equipment purchases and operational profits effectively tax-exempt at the state level. Business Profits Tax (BPT) at 7.5% and Business Enterprise Tax (BET) at 0.55% apply to operating revenue but are substantially lower than neighboring Massachusetts. This combination makes NH one of the most tax-efficient states for data center capital equipment procurement.

tax_incentives.program_name: West Virginia EDGE Act — Data Center Investment Tax Credit
tax_incentives.notes: HB 2002 (2023 Legislative Session), codified at W. Va. Code §11-13Z: Provides a 25% nonrefundable tax credit against corporate net income tax for qualified data center equipment, construction, and infrastructure costs. Capped at $200 million per qualified project. Qualifying data centers must invest at least $25 million and create 25 new direct jobs paying at least 150% of the state average wage. Credit may be carried forward 10 years. Applies to colocation, hyperscale, and enterprise data center projects.

tax_incentives.program_name: Kansas High Performance Incentive Program (HPIP)
tax_incentives.incentive_type: Investment Tax Credit + Sales Tax Refund
tax_incentives.notes: K.S.A. 74-5065: Provides a 10% investment tax credit on qualified capital expenditures (including data center equipment and construction) for companies creating minimum-wage jobs at or above 150% of the county median wage. Companies qualifying for HPIP are also eligible for sales tax refunds on eligible equipment and machinery purchases. The program has no maximum cap but requires pre-application approval. Qualifying data center operators in Johnson and Sedgwick counties have used HPIP for capital equipment credits.

tax_incentives.program_name: Massachusetts Data Processing Equipment Sales Tax Exemption
tax_incentives.notes: M.G.L. ch. 64H §6(r) and (s): Exempts computer equipment and related software used exclusively in data processing from Massachusetts sales and use tax. The exemption applies to servers, storage systems, networking equipment, and cooling systems used in qualifying data center operations. Massachusetts also offers the Economic Development Incentive Program (EDIP) providing investment tax credits and TIF (Tax Increment Financing) for larger qualifying projects. Data center operators in Suffolk, Middlesex, and Norfolk counties regularly apply for EDIP benefits.

tax_incentives.program_name: North Dakota New/Expanding Business Income Tax Exemption
tax_incentives.incentive_type: Income Tax Exemption + Sales Tax Exemption
tax_incentives.notes: N.D.C.C. §57-38.5 (New or Expanding Industry Tax Exemption): Provides a full state income tax exemption for up to 5 years for new or expanding businesses making qualifying capital investments, including data center construction and equipment. Additionally, qualified production and processing equipment used in data center operations may qualify for North Dakota sales tax exemptions under N.D.C.C. §57-39.2-04. The Grand Forks Microsoft campus is among the projects benefiting from these programs. The ND Department of Commerce administers both incentives with the State Tax Commissioner.
